channel.title: ICYMI Law — AI & The Law Link Feed
channel.description: Popular links about AI and the law shared recently on Bluesky.

item.title: OpenAI Sued Over ChatGPT’s ‘Dangerous’ Health Advice
item.description:
The case appears to be the first to argue that a chatbot’s advice harmed someone seeking guidance about a medical condition.

🔥🔥🔥 · reposts: 65 · likes: 208

item.title: I Got Trapped in the AI Ouroboros
item.description:
A foreign news outlet fabricated a quote from me. LLMs have already picked it up.

🔥 · reposts: 35 · likes: 113

item.title: Top American AI Execs Sound Alarm on Chinese Models
item.description:
The White House is divided on how to respond to recent advances in Chinese AI and has weighed crackdown measures.

🔥 · reposts: 19 · likes: 73

item.title: “Privacy for Profits”: ACLU Warns Flock Building a Nationwide Surveillance Network
item.description:
The tech company Flock, makers of traffic cameras that automatically record and log car license plates for law enforcement agencies, is facing increasing scrutiny over accusations that its vast, AI-so...

item.title: Bessent Says US Will Scrutinize Chinese AI Models for IP Theft
item.description:
Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the US will carefully examine open source artificial intelligence models from China for signs of intellectual property theft, amid concerns that a new wave of low...

🔥 · reposts: 30 · likes: 153

item.title: AI and the Commercial Data Loophole
item.description:
The Pentagon’s access to commercial LLMs give it a powerful surveillance tool that could be turned on Americans.

reposts: 61 · likes: 86

item.title: The ACLU Is Arming Lawyers to Expose State Surveillance Secrets
item.description:
A new toolkit for attorneys in Massachusetts targets the technologies police use—and conceal—to build criminal cases, from facial recognition to AI-written police reports.

🔥🔥🔥🔥 · reposts: 209 · likes: 623

item.title: China bans AI “boyfriends” and “girlfriends” over addiction and birth rate concerns - Dexerto
item.description:
China has introduced new regulations effectively banning customizable AI companion apps, forcing users into abrupt digital breakups.

item.title: How LLMs could supercharge mass surveillance in the US
item.description:
The technology could make commercially available bulk datasets even more of a privacy concern.

reposts: 22 · likes: 38

item.title: US judge approves Anthropic's $1.5 billion settlement of copyright lawsuit
item.description:
A federal judge in San Francisco on Monday signed off on artificial intelligence company Anthropic's landmark $1.5 billion settlement of a ​class action lawsuit brought by a group of authors who ‌accused it of misusing their books to train its AI chatbot Claude.

reposts: 21 · likes: 22

item.title: xAI can’t deny Grok makes CSAM anymore. So it’s suing users.
item.description:
Elon Musk's xAI files first lawsuit against Grok user accused of making child sex images.

🔥 · reposts: 8 · likes: 46

item.title: US judge won't block Meta from laying off workers who filed AI discrimination lawsuit
item.description:
A U.S. judge on Friday rejected a bid by 26 employees of Meta Platforms to block the tech giant from laying them off while they pursue claims that they were targeted for job cuts ​by the company's AI-powered tools because they have disabilities or took medical leave.

reposts: 18 · likes: 17

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Indonesia's copyright rewrite puts Google, AI platforms on notice
item.description:
Indonesia is preparing sweeping changes to its copyright law, ​including granting copyright privileges to people who use artificial intelligence to help them generate content, a draft bill reviewed by Reuters showed, setting up a ‌potential showdown between the government and major tech platforms.

reposts: 13 · likes: 26

item.title: Senator Warner Makes a First Foray into Agentic AI Regulation
item.description:
The AI AGENT Act is the first draft federal law to deal with agentic AI power, writes Rutgers legal scholar Ellen P. Goodman.

reposts: 16 · likes: 19

item.title: San Francisco Demands Apple and Google Delete AI ‘Nudify’ Apps From App Stores
item.description:
The City Attorney’s Office sent the tech giants cease-and-desist letters this week telling them to stop profiting from 13 “face-swap” apps that are overwhelmingly used to target women and girls.

item.title: Lawsuit claims Meta's layoff decisions were made by AI, not humans
item.description:
Meta denies using AI to terminate workers with disabilities and medical problems.

item.title: Supreme Court's Cox Ruling Reshapes Copyright Fight, With AI Cases Next in Line
item.description:

item.title: Milwaukee judges banning AI glasses in court as privacy concerns grow
item.description:
Smart glasses are now used to record everything, but that freedom has stopped in state and local courtrooms across the country.

🔥 · reposts: 52 · likes: 272

item.title: Meta used AI to tag workers who took leave to be laid off, lawsuit claims
item.description:
Lawsuit filed by dozens of employees says people who took maternity or disability leave were disproportionately selected for layoffs Dozens of Meta employees have sued the social media company over claims that it used artificial intelligence tools to tag workers for mass layoffs. The workers allege that those AI tools targeted them after they asked for protected or maternity leave or disability accommodation. The lawsuit, filed Monday in federal court in the northern district of California, points to Meta’s workforce reduction of about 8,000 employees earlier this year. Meta is the parent company of Facebook, Instagram and WhatsApp. The suit alleges that Meta used a “constellation of internal artificial intelligence systems”, including AI performance ratings and keystroke- and activity-monitoring data, to pinpoint who to lay off. Continue reading...

reposts: 75 · likes: 160

item.title: Lawsuit Claims the Mayo Clinic's Use of AI Is Butchering Patient Care
item.description:
A former director at the Mayo Clinic claims she was fired in retaliation for reporting the network's horribly inaccurate AI tools.

item.title: Top Paramount Lawyer Claims Opposition To Warner Brothers Merger Is ‘Antisemitic’
item.description:

item.title: Lawyer rebuked for misusing AI again in Roc Nation lawsuit
item.description:
A lawyer who was sanctioned twice for allegedly misusing artificial intelligence has been rebuked again by a federal judge in New York who said the attorney included AI-fabricated quotations in a court filing ​in a lawsuit against entertainment company Roc Nation.

🔥🔥🔥 · reposts: 18 · likes: 93

item.title: A new Illinois law will prohibit the use of AI in teacher evaluations
item.description:

item.title: Apple Claims OpenAI Stole Trade Secrets In New Lawsuit
item.description:
Apple alleges OpenAI improperly obtained trade secrets tied to the iPhone maker’s hardware development through former Apple employees as the AI company expands into consumer devices.

item.title: News outlets urge a judge to sanction OpenAI in a high-stakes AI copyright fight
item.description:
The New York Times, the Daily News and other media outlets are asking a federal judge to impose sanctions on OpenAI, escalating a legal fight over artificial intelligence and copyright that could shape the future of a struggling news industry.

item.title: Apple Is Suing OpenAI for Allegedly Stealing Hardware Secrets
item.description:
The iPhone-maker claims OpenAI encouraged poached employees to bring over confidential presentations, secret prototypes, and key supplier details.

item.title: Apple Sues OpenAI, Accusing It of Stealing Company Secrets (Gift Article)
item.description:

item.title: Class action suit against AI makers over deepfake child sexual abuse material expands
item.description:

item.title: UK regulator warns of "arms race" to keep up with AI use in financial services

item.title: AI startup CEO pleaded guilty in US to trading on insider tips from lawyers
item.description:
The founder ​and chief executive of an AI startup secretly pleaded guilty last year to participating in ‌a vast scheme in which attorneys at major law firms tipped traders about mergers their employers were advising on.

🔥 · reposts: 10 · likes: 11

item.title: Nearly half of early career lawyers use AI daily, survey finds
item.description:
Most early career lawyers are using AI in their work — primarily to conduct legal research and compile information, a new survey has found.

🔥 · reposts: 7 · likes: 15

item.title: Why We Need a 'Truth Campaign' for the AI Era
item.description:
A massive public education campaign is necessary to equip everyone with the knowledge and means to live with AI, write Gaurav Laroia and Charlotte Slaiman.

🔥 · reposts: 5 · likes: 18
